title: Aris-Del Wari
content:

{{Quote|We have opened the child's mind to the larger world of the Force. The child is awakened, and to return it to the birth-parents at this stage would be far too dangerous. It is in everyone's best interests and safety for the child to remain in Jedi custody.|[[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Coleman Trebor]]|<ref name="hnn1" />}}
'''Aris-Del Wari''', born '''Ludi Billane''', was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]] who lived in the [[Time/Legends|era]] known as the [[Great Peace of the Republic]]. Separated from her mother, [[Jonava Billane]], during a quake that wracked the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Domitree]] on [[Ord Thoden]], the child was recovered by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], who discovered that she was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. The Jedi brought the infant into their ranks and gave her the name of "Aris-Del Wari," before introducing her to the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

When Jonava Billane discovered what had happened to her child, she journeyed to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to contest the Jedi's possession of her. In what became known as the [["Baby Ludi" custody case|"Baby Ludi" case]], she petitioned to the Jedi Order for custody of Wari, but the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] refused to return her to her mother. The actions of the Jedi inspired protest rallies and demonstration, and a massive media blitz of the story followed. As events were still unfolding, [[Kailio Entertainments]] prepared to make a [[Untitled Baby Ludi Project|holofilm]] about the case, to be directed by [[Ch'been]]. While Billane was overseeing casting decisions on the film, Wari was transferred to the [[Kamparas Jedi Training Center]], where she would further her connection to the Force.

==Biography==
===Separation===
Ludi Billane was born in [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]] to [[Jonava Billane]], who resided on [[Ord Thoden]]. Mother and daughter were in Ord Thoden's capital of [[Domitree]] when a quake devastated the [[City/Legends|city]]. The pair was separated during the disaster, with the infant left in the ruins of Domitree while Jonava was taken to an outskirt town to recover. Rescue workers from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had been dispatched to Domitree to aid in the recovery, and in their work, they chanced upon Ludi, who was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]].<ref name="hnn1" />

Although normally the Order required parental consent when bringing a child into their ranks, the infant's parents were missing at the time, presumed dead. Naming the unidentified child "Aris-Del Wari," the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] took her back to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where they intended to instruct her in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. One month later, the recovering Jonava was discovered, and she eventually learned what had happened to her child.<ref name="hnn1" />

===Furor===
[[File:LudisMom.jpg|thumb|left|Jonava Billane, holding a holo of her daughter.]]
{{Quote|I believe that a child should be with her mother, and won't stop until I have Ludi back in my arms.|[[Jonava Billane]]|<ref name="hnn1" />}}
When Jonava Billane discovered the fate of her daughter, she exhausted all her funds to travel from Ord Thoden to Coruscant in the hope that she could regain custody over Wari. What followed was a very public [["Baby Ludi" custody case|custody battle]], prompting [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] officials to try to find a resolution to the dispute. Siding with Billane on the issue was the [[People's Inquest]], a populist Jedi-accountability watch-group. The group's leader, [[Thrynka Padaunete]], used the incident as an example of why the actions of the Jedi should be held more accountable to the people who subsidized their operations.

[[Boganni Hrul]], [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of the [[Dynali sector/Legends|Dynali sector]], which encompassed Ord Thoden, submitted a formal petition to the Jedi Order requesting that Wari be returned to Billane, but three weeks later, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] rejected the plea. Councilman [[Coleman Trebor/Legends|Coleman Trebor]] spoke to [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] about the council's decision, explaining that since the Jedi had opened Wari's mind to the Force, it would be too dangerous for to place her back in a non-Jedi environment.

As a result, both the petition itself and its rejection made headlines across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] media. Billane was not deterred, however, and proclaimed that she would not halt her campaign until she had her daughter back.<ref name="hnn1" />

Seven days after the Jedi Order's rejection of Hrul's petition, student activists from the [[University of Coruscant/Legends|University of Coruscant]] overloaded two [[Datanet/Legends|datanet]] hubs with [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] imagery of Wari, who had become widely known as "Baby Ludi." The images spread at a startling rate across the hubs, deleting and replacing existing files. They had been culled from a HoloNet News report, and were accompanied by the caption "I'd rather be with Mom than [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace]]."<ref name="hnn2">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|47|jediwatch/13314_2.html|Baby Ludi Holos Flood UoC Datanet}}</ref> Protest against the Jedi Order's decision in the matter was not exclusive to Coruscant, however. On [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], there was the [[Mothers United March]], and in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], a fund-raising mediathon was held, augmented by the presence of celebrities. In time, Thrynka Padaunete became more directly involved with the case, and took on the role of Billane's appearance manager.<ref name="hnn5" />

====Media coverage====
{{Quote|What have you to hide?|Firris Palbert|<ref name="hnn7">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|jediwatch/13418_1.html|Baby Ludi Moved to Kamparas}}</ref>}}
A week later, it was announced that Billane would be appearing on the [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] talk show, ''[[Essence]]'', to talk about the custody battle over Wari. The show's producer, [[Garox Tronten]], stated his intention to delve into the issue of Wari's custody and hinted toward the idea that the Jedi were in fact "kidnapping and raising an army of mind-controlled youths."<ref name="hnn3">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|48|jediwatch/13321_2.html|Baby Ludi's Mother to Appear on ''Essence''}}</ref> Meanwhile, [[Holodrama/Legends|holofilm]] production company [[Kailio Entertainments]], after beating out two other studio, and paying over 125 million credit to Billane, secured the rights to the "Baby Ludi" story and green-lighted the "[[Untitled Baby Ludi Project]]," a feature-length holo that would cover the dispute. Director of blockbusters such as ''[[Dark Romance]],'' [[Ch'been]] was attached to direct, prompting the spiritualist [[H'drachi]] holomaker to use his "timestream" power to try and predict the future of the case.<ref name="hnn4">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_3.html|Baby Ludi Feature Slated for this Fall}}</ref>

When the Billane episode of ''Essence'' aired, it made news. During the interview, Billane, who was accompanied by Padaunete, pleaded with the Jedi Council to relinquish custody of Wari and thanked everyone who had spoken out against the actions of the Jedi Order. When asked about the holofilm in production, Billane refused to elaborate, but told that a significant portion of the money she got for the rights has gone to charitable ventures and agencies helping her case. After this, Padaunete, changing the subject, used the show as a platform to attack the Jedi, calling them a cult that did not understand the concept of a mother's love. By this time, HoloNet News had come to term the Jedi's taking of the child as a "kidnapping."<ref name="hnn5" />

After the airing of the ''Essence'' episode, active protesting persisted and escalated. Twenty University of Coruscant students from a sociology tour group stormed into the Jedi Temple's public lobby and began throwing pre-programmed [[graffiti-bomb]]s. The bombs, when detonated, painted messages such as "Baby Ludi Wants Her Mom," and "Broodsnatchers!" across the lobby. Two Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] attempted to quell the protest, but were unsuccessful. The students were able to push into the Temple's Second Atrium Lobby before being [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricked]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] and subsequently subdued and taken off-site by [[Judicial Forces/Legends|authorities]]. The Council refused to comment on the incident, and Billane was busy, by this stage, reviewing casting decisions for Ch'been's holofilm. Padaunete acknowledged the efforts of the students on HoloNet News, however.<ref name="hnn6">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|51|jediwatch/13411_1.html|Activists Deface Jedi Temple Lobby}}</ref>

Per standard procedure, the fourteen-month-old Wari and nineteen of her fellow [[Clan (Jedi)|clan]] members were transferred to the [[Kamparas]] [[Kamparas Jedi Training Center|Jedi Training Center]]. People's Inquest members who had been camping outside the Jedi Temple soon learned of the move, and launched into an impromptu rally. The Inquest's acting leader, [[Firris Palbert]], publicly decried the Jedi for the transfer and urged his fellow protesters to journey to Kamparas, where they could continue their opposition. At this stage, Billane, along with Padaunete, did not comment the events, since both of them were still reviewing casting decisions for the Baby Ludi holofilm on Kassido.<ref name="hnn7" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aris-Del Wari was created for [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]], written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]. In his original pitch for the HoloNet News website, Hidalgo called the "Baby Ludi" story a trivial one that the media would latch on to in the face of the impending [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]]. He compared the fixation on the story to the real-life examples of [[Wikipedia:Elián González affair|Elián González]], [[Wikipedia:O. J. Simpson murder case|O. J. Simpson]], and [[Wikipedia:JonBenét Ramsey|JonBenét Ramsey]].<ref name="blog">{{Blog|pablog|141|Dusting Off the Archives: Original HoloNet News Pitch}}</ref> Ludi's story was spread across seven issues of HoloNet News, which were published in the weeks leading up to the release of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']].
